task_completion.description: Open the NCEP precipitation dataset in a visualization tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data visualization tool (such as Panoply) is open, showing the loaded dataset 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' and the 'prate' (precipitation rate) variable.

task_completion.description: Visualize the precipitation difference between May and September over the US Midwest.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A map plot is visible showing a mathematical difference between two time steps (May and September, typically time step 5 and 9) for the 'prate' variable. The map should be zoomed or focused on the US Midwest (approx 25N-50N, 105W-80W).

task_completion.description: Export the May-September precipitation difference map as a PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows the plot being saved as 'midwest_precip_deficit_may_sep.png' in the '~/Documents/MississippiLogistics' directory.

task_completion.description: Visualize the September precipitation over the US Midwest.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A map plot is visible showing the precipitation data specifically for September (typically time step 9) focused on the US Midwest.

task_completion.description: Export the September precipitation map as a PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows the plot being saved as 'midwest_precip_september.png' in the '~/Documents/MississippiLogistics' directory.

task_completion.description: Write a logistics report based on the briefing and precipitation data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a written report discussing Mississippi River logistics, draft restrictions, and autumn precipitation. The file is saved as 'draft_restriction_report.txt' in the '~/Documents/MississippiLogistics' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually generated the plots using a visualization tool or script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the plot settings, map projections, or array combinations in a tool like Panoply, rather than just downloading or copying pre-existing images.

integrity.description: Verify the agent composed the logistics report in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing or pasting meaningful text into a text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, vim) for 'draft_restriction_report.txt', rather than just creating an empty file via terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the provided NCEP dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visualization tool should clearly display 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' as the active source file, ensuring the agent didn't load a different or synthetic dataset.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target Midwest coordinates are approximately 25N to 50N, 105W to 80W. For the May minus September precipitation difference map, May is generally wetter than September in this region (median difference +0.83 mm/day), so the difference map should visually indicate positive values over most of the Midwest. The dataset variable is 'prate'. All outputs must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Documents/MississippiLogistics/.
